name: HISTORY
describeDifficult: A gentle introduction to the archives.
Only the sharpest minds will be able to unravel the past.
The ultimate challenge for true experts.

questions.question: Which French emperor lost the Battle of Waterloo ?
Which famous text was adopted on August 26, 1789, in France ?
choices: The Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen

questions.question: Which Ottoman sultan conquered Constantinople in 1453 ?
Which Ottoman ruler, nicknamed 'the Magnificent' by Europeans, brought the Empire to its peak in the 16th century ?
In what year did the Belgian Revolution take place ?
In which region was the printing press invented ?
What was the Enigma machine used for during World War II ?
choices: To encrypt and decrypt secret messages
To detect enemy submarines using ultrasound
To calculate the trajectory of V2 missiles
To transmit propaganda images via radio waves.

questions.question: In what year did television broadcast color images for the first time in Belgium ?

name: INFORMATIC
describeDifficult: Ready to try your hand at the basics of computing ?
You know the basics… but is that enough ?

questions.question: What does the acronym 'RAM' stand for ?
In what programming language was the Linux kernel written ?
What is my network's IP address? (ex : 192.128.1.15 )
Who is considered the first computer programmer in history ?
A programmer's job consists of :
choices: Exclusively managing the sale of computer hardware in a store.
Monitoring building security through surveillance cameras.
Writing and testing code to create software or applications.

questions.question: What is a programming language ?
choices: A foreign language used only by network engineers.
A software used to translate texts from one language to another.
A dictionary of technical words for repairing keyboards.
A code allowing a human to give instructions to a machine.

questions.question: What is the difference between a bit and a byte ?
choices: A bit is used for text and a byte is used only for images.
A byte is a hardware part while a bit is a Wi-Fi signal.
A bit is the minimum unit (0 or 1), while a byte is a group of 8 bits.
There is no difference; they are two words for the same thing.

questions.question: What is the major difference between RAM and ROM ?
choices: RAM is volatile (erases without power) while ROM is permanent.
RAM is used to store photos and ROM is used to run games.
RAM is an external component (USB flash drive) and ROM is internal to the computer.
RAM is much slower than ROM for reading information.

name: DIVERTISSMENT
describeDifficult: Perfect for getting started in the world of entertainment.
There are quite a few surprises in store for you here.
The classics won’t be enough anymore, we need to go further.
This requires a real grasp of pop culture.

questions.question: Which plumber is Nintendo's mascot ?
Which Swedish music streaming service dominated the market in the 2010s ?
Which actor plays Jack Sparrow in Pirates of the Caribbean ?
In what year was the first iPhone released ?
Who is the NBA player with the most championships ?
What is the most watched sport on the planet ?
What is the most expensive transfer fee in football history ?
Why was Mike Tyson, aka Iron Mike, disqualified during his fight against Evander Holyfield in 1997 ?
choices: He accidentally knocked out the referee.
He kept hitting after the bell.

name: TOURISM
describeDifficult: We’re starting off gently – perfect for exploring new horizons.
We're venturing off the beaten track a bit.
Things are getting serious when it comes to tourism.

questions.question: In which country is Machu Picchu located ?
What is the capital of Japan ??
Which country is historically the world's top tourist destination by number of visitors ?
Which monument was built by an emperor in memory of his wife ?
Which country has the most islands in the world ?
In which country is Mont Blanc located ?
Which is the largest mosque in Africa ?
Where do 'French Fries' come from ?
